title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Pizza Movie," featuring Gaten Matarazzo and Sean Giambrone, is streaming on Hulu, depicting a zany pizza quest.
"Sirat," an Oscar-nominated film exploring a father-son journey in Morocco, is available on Hulu.
"The Testaments," a sequel to "The Handmaid's Tale," is now streaming on Hulu with Ann Dowd reprising her role.

title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum is out for the Celtics' game against Pelicans due to right Achilles repair management.
Jaylen Brown, managing left Achilles tendinitis, is available to play against the Pelicans.
Three other Celtics starters, Hauser, Queta, and White, are listed as probable but expected to play.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson from Concord is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her daughter Emma's cancer journey.
Emma, Robertson's 16-year-old daughter, was diagnosed with medulloblastoma requiring urgent surgery and extensive treatment.
Robertson's participation signifies both a personal milestone and tribute to the medical community supporting Emma's fight.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens will not debut for the Bruins against the Tampa Bay Lightning on Saturday.
Marco Sturm prioritizes patience for Hagens, emphasizing the need for the 19-year-old to acclimate to the team's system.
Hagens practiced on a 'fifth' line, but participated in second power-play unit reps ahead of the game.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Forbes lists highlight Boston billionaires Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers as top self-made Americans.
Historical self-made Americans list includes Boston ties like Benjamin Franklin, Babe Ruth, and Louisa May Alcott.
Rankings consider financial success, obstacles overcome, and long-term impact, scoring only those achieving a 9 or 10.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon will take place on April 20, 2026, starting in Hopkinton and ending at Copley Square.
This year, the marathon features 30,000 participants from over 130 countries and introduces six start waves instead of four.
Live coverage begins at 4 a.m. on WCVB Channel 5, with streaming available through the Very Local app.

title: Patriots reportedly adding former Titans assistant Shane Bowen to coaching staff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots are hiring Shane Bowen as an analyst, who was the defensive coordinator for the Giants and Titans.
Bowen has previously worked with head coach Mike Vrabel at the Titans, Texans, and Ohio State.
Zak Kuhr is the new Patriots defensive coordinator, while Terrell Williams returns as assistant head coach.

title: Where are the public restrooms in Boston?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston offers public restrooms in libraries, community centers, and some firehouses open 24/7, like Engine 29 at 138 Chestnut Hill Ave.
Massachusetts law allows patients with certain medical conditions access to employee-only restrooms when nearby public options are unavailable.
The Age Strong Commission provides an interactive map of public restrooms in Boston, highlighting distribution by neighborhoods and access features.

title: Masconomet school officials did not adequately respond to harassment claims, student athlete says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Charlotte Rollins, a Masconomet High School freshman, accused administrators of inadequately addressing harassment, including threats of sexual violence.
Rollins alleged the school ignored reports of bullying, cheating, underage drinking, and retaliatory behavior from male athletes.
Teachers and staff expressed concern over the administration's inaction, calling for immediate intervention by the School Committee.

title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 million extra at the pump since the Iran war began
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts drivers paid over $137 million more for gas since Iran conflict began in February, per Congressional report.
New England families have spent over $200 extra on home heating since the war started, report states.
Massachusetts gubernatorial candidates support suspending gas tax to mitigate costs; Gov. Healey opposes tax suspension.
